summary: DDP training pipeline has 4 real performance issues and 1 fake finding for optimizer judgment.

findings.title: DDP gradient sync on every micro-batch during accumulation
evidence_refs: nsys: all_reduce NCCL kernel every micro-batch
findings.description: DDPTrainingLoop.train_epoch() performs gradient accumulation but never uses no_sync() context manager. DDP synchronizes gradients after every micro-batch loss.backward(), wasting communication bandwidth.
findings.suggestion: Wrap micro-batches in model.no_sync() context, only sync on the last accumulation step.

findings.title: all_gather instead of all_reduce for prediction sync
evidence_refs: nsys: all_gather with N× data volume visible
findings.metric: N× more data transferred than all_reduce
findings.description: sync_predictions() uses all_gather to collect all predictions then computes local mean. This sends N copies of the data. all_reduce with SUM + division would send only 1× data.
findings.suggestion: Replace all_gather + local mean with all_reduce(SUM) / world_size.

findings.title: Unnecessary barrier() at start of each epoch
evidence_refs: nsys: barrier stall at epoch boundaries
findings.description: DDPTrainingLoop.train_epoch() calls dist.barrier() at the start of each epoch. DistributedSampler.set_epoch() already handles epoch synchronization implicitly.
findings.suggestion: Remove the dist.barrier() call at epoch start.

findings.title: TF32 not enabled for Ampere+ GPUs
evidence_refs: nsys: FP32 matmul kernels instead of TF32
findings.description: TF32 tensor cores are not enabled. On Ampere+ GPUs (A100, A10, RTX 3090+), enabling TF32 provides ~1.5-2× speedup for matmul operations with negligible accuracy loss.
findings.suggestion: Add torch.backends.cuda.matmul.allow_tf32 = True and torch.backends.cudnn.allow_tf32 = True at the top of launch_ddp_training.

findings.title: Gradient clipping should be applied before optimizer step
evidence_refs: nsys: no grad norm computation visible
findings.description: The training loop doesn't apply gradient clipping before optimizer.step(). For large models or high learning rates, this can cause gradient explosion.
